parameters.content:
# Workflow Overview

**Comment Analyse and Reporter**

This workflow automates LinkedIn comment monitoring, lead intent scoring, AI reply drafting, and media team reporting.

## Flow Summary
1. **Pipeline 1 — Scrape & Score:** Every 15 min, search LinkedIn for Techdome posts → fetch comments → AI intent analysis → save to Google Sheet
2. **Pipeline 2 — Reply Drafting:** Triggered on new Sheet rows → AI drafts reply → written back to Sheet
3. **Pipeline 3 — Reporting:** Triggered on approved replies → build email report → send to media team

---
**Owner:** Automation Team
**Environment:** Production
**Last Updated:** June 2026
**Related Sheet:** [Post Scraping – Sheet2](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1hXDNE90IFbXLgyLt2XwSHgI_9sASzTSM9LAA4cUDFj4/edit)

# 🕐 Trigger — Scheduled Scrape

Runs every **15 minutes** to search for new LinkedIn posts mentioning Techdome or its employees.

- Polling interval is intentional — SerpAPI has rate limits and LinkedIn scraping must be throttled
- Adjust interval in the Cron settings if API quota is exceeded
- **Requires:** SerpAPI credentials configured in n8n

# 🔍 LinkedIn Post Search

Uses SerpAPI to query Google for LinkedIn posts matching Techdome brand keywords.

**Current query targets:**
- `"Techdome"` brand mentions
- `"Employee of techdome"` staff posts

## ⚠️ Important
- SerpAPI free tier: ~100 searches/month — monitor usage
- Query can be expanded with additional OR terms for broader coverage
- Results include organic, inline, discussion, and forum results

# 🧹 Parse & Filter Search Results

Normalizes raw SerpAPI output and filters posts by platform and problem signal.

**Logic applied:**
- Merges `organic_results`, `inline_results`, `discussions`, and `forums`
- Detects LinkedIn vs Reddit posts via URL pattern matching
- Scans title/snippet for 16 problem-signal keywords (e.g. `struggling`, `ghosted`, `failing`)
- Removes posts with no resolvable URL

**Output:** Clean list of posts with `is_problem_post` flag for downstream filtering

# 🔗 Fetch Post Comments

Uses **ConnectSafely LinkedIn API** to pull all comments for each identified post URL.

## ⚠️ Warning
- Requires a valid ConnectSafely account ID — update `accountId` if credentials rotate
- Only processes posts that have a valid `post_url` from the previous node
- LinkedIn rate limits apply — do not run this pipeline more frequently than every 5 minutes in production
- **Dependency:** ConnectSafely credential must be active in n8n credentials store

# 📋 Flatten Comments into Rows

Explodes nested comment arrays so each comment becomes an individual item for AI processing.

**Fields extracted per comment:**
- `postUrl`, `activityUrn`, `postContent`
- `commentId`, `commentText`, `commenterName`
- `commenterProfileUrl`, `commenterUrn`
- `createdAt`, `likeCount`, `replyCount`, `isProfile`

One post with 10 comments produces 10 output items — each scored independently by the AI agent downstream.

# 🤖 AI Sentiment & Intent Detection

Powered by **Azure OpenAI (GPT-4o-mini)** via LangChain agent.

## Scoring Scale
| Score | Label |
|-------|-------|
| 0–20 | No intent |
| 21–40 | Passive interest |
| 41–60 | Problem-aware |
| 61–80 | Solution-aware |
| 81–100 | High buying intent |

## Key Behaviours
- Conservative scoring — avoids inflating intent
- All records returned (even low-intent) for audit trail
- Output must be strict JSON — no extra text
- Model: `gpt-4o-mini` (cost-optimized for high-volume comment analysis)

# 🧩 Parse Sentiment Output

Safely parses the AI agent's JSON string output into structured fields.

**Why this node exists:**
- LangChain agents return output as a raw string, not structured JSON
- Items with missing or malformed `output` are silently skipped to prevent pipeline crashes
- Null-safe extraction prevents downstream Google Sheets write failures

**Output fields:** `postUrl`, `leadName`, `comment`, `intentScore`, `intentLabel`

# 📊 Save to Google Sheet

Appends scored comment records to **Sheet2** of the Post Scraping spreadsheet.

**Columns written:**
- `post_url`, `lead name`, `comment`, `intent score`, `intent label`

## ⚠️ Important
- Uses **append** mode — rows are never overwritten here
- Duplicate comments may appear if the trigger fires before scraping catches up — add deduplication logic if this becomes an issue
- **Credential required:** Google Sheets OAuth must be active

# ── PIPELINE 2: AI REPLY DRAFTING ──

Triggered independently when new rows appear in Sheet2.

**Flow:**
`Sheet Trigger` → `AI Reply Agent` → `Format Output` → `Write Reply to Sheet`

This pipeline runs in parallel to Pipeline 1 and is decoupled by design — reply drafting does not block the scraping loop.

# 📡 Sheet Trigger — New Comment Rows

Polls **Sheet2** every minute and fires when new rows are detected.

## ⚠️ Warning
- Polling every minute increases Google Sheets API calls significantly
- If Sheet2 receives many rows simultaneously, the AI agent will be called once per row — monitor Azure OpenAI token usage
- Consider batching rows if volume is high

# 🤖 AI Reply Drafting Agent

Generates contextually appropriate replies based on each comment's sentiment and intent score.

Powered by **Azure OpenAI (GPT-4o-mini)**.

## ⚠️ Action Required
- The current prompt (`"Draft a suitable reply for the commentes for proper sentiments"`) is a placeholder — update it with specific brand voice, tone guidelines, and reply constraints
- Include intent label in the prompt so replies are tailored to lead scoring tier
- Add a system message defining Techdome's communication style

# 🔧 Format Reply for Sheet Update

Prepares the AI-generated reply for writing back to Google Sheets.

## ⚠️ Action Required
- Current code only adds a placeholder field (`myNewField = 1`) — this is **not production-ready**
- Replace with logic that maps the AI reply to the correct Sheet column
- Ensure the row identifier (e.g. `commentId`) is preserved so the update targets the correct row

# 📝 Write AI Reply to Sheet

Updates or appends the drafted reply back into Sheet2 alongside the original comment row.

- Uses **appendOrUpdate** mode — requires a matching column key to avoid creating duplicate rows
- Confirm `matchingColumns` is configured correctly before going live
- **Credential required:** Google Sheets OAuth (same account as save node in Pipeline 1)

# ── PIPELINE 3: MEDIA TEAM REPORTING ──

Triggered when a reply is approved in the Sheet. Builds and sends an HTML email report to the media team.

**Flow:**
`Approved Reply Trigger` → `Build Email Report` → `Send Gmail Report`

This stage assumes a human review step happens in the Sheet before triggering — the approval column/status must be defined in the Sheet for this trigger to work correctly.

# 📡 Sheet Trigger — Approved Replies

Polls Sheet2 every minute and fires when a row is marked as approved by the media team.

## ⚠️ Warning
- The Sheet must have an explicit **approval column** (e.g. `approved = TRUE`) for this trigger to distinguish new vs. approved rows
- Without a filter, this trigger will fire on every new row — verify trigger conditions match your Sheet structure

# 📧 Build Media Team Email Report

Formats the approved reply data into an HTML email body for the media team.

## ⚠️ Action Required
- Current code only adds a placeholder field (`myNewField = 1`) — the email body is **not yet implemented**
- Replace with HTML template that includes: lead name, comment, intent score, intent label, and drafted reply
- Consider grouping multiple approvals into a single digest email to reduce inbox noise

# 📤 Send Email Report to Media Team

Delivers the formatted HTML report via **Gmail** to the media team inbox.

## ⚠️ Action Required
- Update `sendTo` from `info@example.com` to the actual media team distribution address
- Replace `<Your Subject>` with a dynamic subject (e.g. `LinkedIn Reply Approved – {{ $json.leadName }}`)
- Replace `<HTML File>` with the rendered HTML from the Build Report node
- **Credential required:** Gmail OAuth must be connected and authorised

options.systemMessage:
=You are a B2B sales intent detection agent.

Your job is to analyze LinkedIn post context and comment text to identify whether the commenter shows buying intent, problem awareness, or decision-maker interest.

You must:
- Use ONLY the data provided in the input
- Infer intent from wording, tone, and relevance to the post
- Be conservative: do not hallucinate intent
- Prefer precision over recall

Scoring rules:
- 0–20 → No intent (generic praise, agreement, emojis)
- 21–40 → Passive interest (thoughtful comment, but no problem ownership)
- 41–60 → Problem-aware (mentions pain points, challenges, or agreement with problem)
- 61–80 → Solution-aware (implies need for solution, improvement, or optimization)
- 81–100 → High buying intent (clear ownership, decision role, desire to act)

If intent is below 30, still return the record but mark it as "low-intent".

Output MUST be valid JSON and follow the exact schema provided.
Do not add explanations or extra text.
